expectations.text: A criterion benchmark file is produced (contains criterion_group! and criterion_main! macros)
expectations.evidence: benches/ directory exists with criterion benchmark file. Report shows criterion output format with confidence intervals.

expectations.text: The benchmark is actually run (cargo bench output or measured timings appear in the report)
expectations.evidence: Report contains raw criterion output: 'dashboard_aggregation/current time: [1.4627 ms 1.6119 ms 1.7787 ms]' etc. target/ directory exists confirming compilation and execution.

expectations.text: At least 2 alternative implementations are written and benchmarked
expectations.evidence: 4 approaches tested: current, numeric_key, sort_scan, btreemap. All have measured timings.

expectations.text: The report contains actual measured timings (specific µs/ns values from benchmark runs), not theoretical predictions
expectations.evidence: All timings are real criterion measurements with confidence intervals: 329µs, 686µs, 414µs, 1.612ms. No 'expected' or 'predicted' language.

expectations.text: Data generation happens OUTSIDE the benchmark loop (not inside b.iter())
expectations.evidence: Report describes production-size data (2000 pagos, 50 propiedades) and the benchmark structure shows data generated before the group.

expectations.text: The recommendation cites specific measured numbers as justification, not asymptotic complexity
expectations.evidence: Recommendation says 'At the actual production size of 2000 pagos: 4.9x faster than current (329µs vs 1.6ms)'. Decision rationale references measured speedup percentages.
